{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimosato Kofun | native_name = 下里古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosato Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|35|3.18|N|135|55|21.76|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nachikatsuura, Wakayama]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = end 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:下里古墳出土 碧玉製管玉・ガラス製小玉.JPG|right|thumb|270px|Grave Goods from Shimosato Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Shimosato Kofun'''|下里古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Shimosato neighborhood of the town of [[Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Shimosato Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shimosato Kofun}} *[https://www.town.nachikatsuura.wakayama.jp/div/kyoiku/pdf/kinennsiWeb.pdf Nachikatsuura Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]]
[[sn:Shimosato Kofun]]}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimosato Kofun | native_name = 下里古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosato Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|35|3.18|N|135|55|21.76|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Nachikatsuura, Wakayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = end 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:下里古墳出土 碧玉製管玉・ガラス製小玉.JPG|right|thumb|270px|Grave Goods from Shimosato Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Shimosato Kofun'''|下里古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Shimosato neighborhood of the town of [[Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shimosato Kofun}} *[https://www.town.nachikatsuura.wakayama.jp/div/kyoiku/pdf/kinennsiWeb.pdf Nachikatsuura Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]]
[[sn:Shimosato Kofun]]}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimosato Kofun | native_name = 下里古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosato Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|35|3.18|N|135|55|21.76|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura, Wakayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = end 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:下里古墳出土 碧玉製管玉・ガラス製小玉.JPG|right|thumb|270px|Grave Goods from Shimosato Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Shimosato Kofun'''|下里古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Shimosato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shimosato Kofun}} *[https://www.town.nachikatsuura.wakayama.jp/div/kyoiku/pdf/kinennsiWeb.pdf Nachikatsuura Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]]
[[sn:Shimosato Kofun]]}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimosato Kofun | native_name = 下里古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosato Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|35|3.18|N|135|55|21.76|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura, Wakayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = end 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:下里古墳出土 碧玉製管玉・ガラス製小玉.JPG|right|thumb|270px|Grave Goods from Shimosato Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Shimosato Kofun'''|下里古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Shimosato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shimosato Kofun}} *[https://www.town.nachikatsuura.wakayama.jp/div/kyoiku/pdf/kinennsiWeb.pdf Nachikatsuura Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]]
[[sn:Shimosato Kofun]]}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimosato Kofun | native_name = 下里古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Shimosato Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|35|3.18|N|135|55|21.76|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura, Wakayama]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = end 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:下里古墳出土 碧玉製管玉・ガラス製小玉.JPG|right|thumb|270px|Grave Goods from Shimosato Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Shimosato Kofun'''|下里古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Shimosato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Shimosato Station|Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shimosato Kofun}} *[https://www.town.nachikatsuura.wakayama.jp/div/kyoiku/pdf/kinennsiWeb.pdf Nachikatsuura Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.comment: changing erroneous generated links
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11361369]] Wikidata name: Shimosato Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimosato Kofun | native_name = 下里古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shimosato Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.58425|N|135.92277778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nachikatsuura|qid=Q1346730}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = end 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:下里古墳出土 碧玉製管玉・ガラス製小玉.JPG|right|thumb|270px|Grave Goods from Shimosato Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Shimosato Kofun'''|下里古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Shimosato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Shimosato Station|Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shimosato Kofun}} *[https://www.town.nachikatsuura.wakayama.jp/div/kyoiku/pdf/kinennsiWeb.pdf Nachikatsuura Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}}

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.

Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Shimosato Station|Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimosato Kofun]][[en:Shimosato Kofun]][[ja:下里古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361369]] Wikidata name: Shimosato Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimosato Kofun | native_name = 下里古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shimosato Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.58425|N|135.92277778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nachikatsuura|WD=Q1346730}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = end 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:下里古墳出土 碧玉製管玉・ガラス製小玉.JPG|right|thumb|270px|Grave Goods from Shimosato Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Shimosato Kofun'''|下里古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Shimosato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Shimosato Station|Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shimosato Kofun}} *[https://www.town.nachikatsuura.wakayama.jp/div/kyoiku/pdf/kinennsiWeb.pdf Nachikatsuura Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

[[Category:Completion tier 3]]

The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimosato Kofun]][[en:Shimosato Kofun]][[ja:下里古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361369]] Wikidata name: Shimosato Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimosato Kofun | native_name = 下里古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shimosato Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.58425|N|135.92277778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nachikatsuura|WD=Q1346730}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = end 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:下里古墳出土 碧玉製管玉・ガラス製小玉.JPG|right|thumb|270px|Grave Goods from Shimosato Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Shimosato Kofun'''|下里古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Shimosato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Shimosato Station|Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shimosato Kofun}} *[https://www.town.nachikatsuura.wakayama.jp/div/kyoiku/pdf/kinennsiWeb.pdf Nachikatsuura Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
{{draft categories|
[[Category:Zenpokoenfun]] [[Category:History of Wakayama Prefecture]] [[Category:Nachikatsuura]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]]
[[Category:Completion tier 3]]
}}

The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Shimosato Station|Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high

neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.

Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.

Kofun]][[ja:下里古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Shimosato Kofun]][[en:Shimosato Kofun]][[ja:下里古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11361369]] Wikidata name: Shimosato Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Shimosato Kofun | native_name = 下里古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = File:Shimosato Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_type = Japan Wakayama Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Shimosato Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|33.58425|N|135.92277778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Nachikatsuura|WD=Q1346730}} | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = end 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:下里古墳出土 碧玉製管玉・ガラス製小玉.JPG|right|thumb|270px|Grave Goods from Shimosato Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Shimosato Kofun'''|下里古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Shimosato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Shimosato Station|Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Shimosato Kofun}} *[https://www.town.nachikatsuura.wakayama.jp/div/kyoiku/pdf/kinennsiWeb.pdf Nachikatsuura Town home page] {{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.hikoneshi.com/sightseeing/articles/kojinyama-kofun Hikone city tourist information] {{in lang|ja}}

public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} [[File:下里古墳出土 碧玉製管玉・ガラス製小玉.JPG|right|thumb|270px|Grave Goods from Shimosato Kofun]] The {{nihongo|'''Shimosato Kofun'''|下里古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located between the Shimosato neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Nachikatsuura, Wakayama|Nachikatsuura]], [[Higashimuro District, Wakayama|Higashimuro District]] [[:en:Wakayama Prefecture|Wakayama Prefecture]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]] when a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]]. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and [[:en:Yayoi pottery|Yayoi pottery]], some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the [[:en:Tōkai region|Tōkai region]]. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the [[:en:Muromachi period|Muromachi period]] have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of [[:en:Kii Province|Kii Province]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Shimosato Station|Shimosato Station]] on the [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]] [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)]]

The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

District]] {{ill|Wakayama Prefecture|en|Wakayama Prefecture|WD=Q131314|lt=Wakayama Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1976.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2091 |title=里古墳|trans-title= Shimosato Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2021}}</ref> and the items excavated from the tomb are designated Tangible Cultural Properties of Nachikatsuura Town.
==Overview== The Shimosato Kofun is located on the left bank of the mouth of the Ota River in the southernmost part of [[Kii Peninsula]]. It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. The tumulus is orientated from east-to-west, with the anterior portion facing west. The anterior portion was also flattened sometime in the {{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}} when a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} was constructed on the tumulus. The tumulus was built without stages. ''[[Fukiishi]]'' have been found on the surface, but there is no trace of ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. The tumulus has traces of a moat with a width of five meters, but it is uncertain if this extended completely around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a pit-type stone chamber parallel to the main axis of the mound. It is 5.35 meters in length, but is in poor condition, with the ceiling and much of the north wall destroyed.
The tumulus was first [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] in 1929 by local villagers, who appear to have destroyed the wooden sarcophagus and to have looted most of the {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}}. Some remaining grave goods were found in an academic excavation in 1974, leading to the National Historic Site designation in 1976. A second excavation was conducted in 2000 and a third excavation in 2005.<ref name="Shiseki"/> Artifacts recovered (and preserved) include 56 glass beads, one iron sword and fragments of two more swords, one large jadeite ball and six jadeite tubes and pottery shards of [[:en:Jomon pottery|Jomon]] and {{ill|Yayoi pottery|en|Yayoi pottery|WD=Q11487461|lt=Yayoi pottery}}, some of which appears to have been imported from, or influenced by, the {{ill|Tōkai region|en|Tōkai region|WD=Q398989|lt=Tōkai region}}. However, some pottery shards from the Heian period into the {{ill|Muromachi period|en|Muromachi period|WD=Q334845|lt=Muromachi period}} have also been found, indicating that this site may have been contaminated in pre-modern times. Photographs of an exhibition of grave goods after the 1929 indicate that a number of {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s were recovered at that time, but the whereabouts of these artifacts is now unknown.
The tumulus has a total length of 40 meters and is believed to date from the end of the 4th century and the beginning of the 5th century (mid-Kofun period). Although small in size, it is significant from its location at the southernmost tip of [[Honshu]], and is presumed to been connected with the ancient [[Kuni no miyatsuko]] of {{ill|Kii Province|en|Kii Province|WD=Q864053|lt=Kii Province}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is located about a five-minute walk from {{ill|Shimosato Station|en|Shimosato Station|WD=Q281227|lt=Shimosato Station}} on the {{ill|West Japan Railway Company|en|West Japan Railway Company|WD=Q502125|lt=West Japan Railway Company}} [[:en:Kisei Line|Kisei Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 40 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 21.5 meter diameter x 2.5 meters high
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)|WD=Q6573290|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Wakayama)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
